text.title: Shared Infra Capacity Board Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every shared-infra capacity board must enforce so it surfaces capacity demand instead of merely logging it.

rule.statement: Every row MUST carry four required fields: resource (named), team, projected_delta (numeric with unit), need_by_date (concrete date). Free-form notes belong in a separate column, not in lieu of these four.
rule.rationale: Cross-team architects fight over shared infra because each team plans capacity in isolation; without typed numeric demand, the weekly sync degenerates into vibes. Required typed fields make conflicts arithmetic, not rhetorical.

example.code: - resource: redis-prod team: checkout projected_delta: "+12 GB memory" need_by_date: 2026-06-10 owner: "@alex.kim" note: "Cart-session TTL increase per spec-1024"

rule.statement: The board MUST be updated weekly, on the same recurring day, before the architecture sync; stale rows (last_updated >14 days) are flagged for archival.
rule.rationale: An untouched capacity board is decoration. Pinning updates to the sync forces decisions to consume the artefact, which keeps the artefact alive.

rule.statement: Every row MUST name a single accountable owner (handle) — not the team. No bare "team-name" entries.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. When two teams' projections collide on the same resource, the resolution needs two named humans, not two team names that nobody can DM.

rule.statement: When two rows project conflicting demand on the same resource within overlapping windows, the conflict MUST be tagged "CONFLICT" and assigned a resolution owner before the sync ends.
rule.rationale: The board's only job is to surface fights early; if conflicts get noted but not assigned, they re-emerge at deploy time exactly as before. The tag + owner + deadline turn surfacing into action.

rule.statement: Closed or resolved rows MUST be moved to an archive section with the realised outcome (delivered / cancelled / partially-met) and a one-line learning note; rows are never silently deleted.
rule.rationale: The archive becomes the ground truth for next quarter's capacity-planning calibration — teams who consistently over-estimate or under-estimate are identifiable only with the history.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
